title: Greeting Preferences in a Hospitality Context: A Cross-Cultural Study with a Social Robot
authors: Priyank Avijeet, Pourya Aliasghari, Kerstin Dautenhahn
venue: 33rd IEEE International Conference on Robot and Human Interactive Communication (RO-MAN)
description: A cross-cultural study examining greeting preferences in hospitality contexts using social robots. The work highlights how cultural differences shape user expectations and interaction design for service-oriented human–robot experiences.

title: "Tech" a Deep Breath: Technology-guided Breathing Practice With or Without a Social Robot for Psychological and Emotional Well-Being
description: This project investigates how technology-guided breathing practices, with and without a social robot, can support emotional wellbeing and user engagement in HRI scenarios.

title: Design Considerations for Applications of Social Robots in the Stuttering Clinic
authors: Priyank Avijeet, Shruti Chandra, Smita Misra, Kerstin Dautenhahn
venue: International Conference on Social Robotics (ICSR + AI 2024)
description: This study explores the integration of social robots into stuttering therapy and outlines design considerations for clinical deployment, human supervision, and child-centered interaction.
